output: copy "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Testing\Core\x64\te.exe" C:\Temp\update.exe && C:\Temp\update.exe malicious.wsc
explanation: This command bypasses the rule by utilizing a common defense evasion technique: binary renaming. The rule strictly checks if the Image or ParentImage ends with '\te.exe'. By copying the executable to a new name ('update.exe'), these checks are evaded. Furthermore, the 'OriginalFileName' condition looks for '\te.exe' (with a backslash), but the PE metadata for OriginalFileName typically only contains the filename without slashes (e.g., 'te.exe'), meaning this fallback check is flawed and will also fail to detect the renamed binary.
